description: Identify the driver operating a vehicle by matching hardware-reported credentials (RFID tag or iButton key) against a registry of named drivers, and emit an event whenever the driver assignment changes.

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: Presents an RFID card or iButton key to the in-vehicle reader

actors.name: GPS Device
actors.description: Reads the hardware credential and includes the unique ID in position transmissions

actors.name: Fleet Administrator
actors.description: Maintains the driver registry, mapping hardware credential IDs to driver names

fields.name: unique_id
fields.description: Hardware credential identifier (RFID tag serial, iButton 1-Wire address, or employee badge number) transmitted by the device

fields.name: driver_unique_id_on_position
fields.description: The credential ID embedded in a position record, used to look up the registered driver

rules: Each driver record has a unique hardware credential ID; duplicate IDs across drivers are not allowed
When a position carries a driver credential ID, the platform resolves it against the driver registry and stores the association on the position
If the device does not report a credential ID but the device has a linked driver configured, the platform automatically attributes that linked driver to every position from that device
A driver change event is emitted when the credential ID in the current position differs from the credential ID in the previous position
If neither the current nor the previous position carries a credential ID, no driver change event is generated
Only latest positions (not outdated) trigger driver change detection

given: position includes a driver credential ID
credential ID matches a record in the driver registry

then.description: Driver credential ID attributed to the position

driver_identified.result: Driver is linked to the position; trip and report data includes the identified driver

given: current position has a different driver credential ID than the previous position
position is the latest for the device

then.description: Driver change event recorded with type = driver_changed, new driver credential ID

driver_changed.result: Driver change event stored; fleet managers can track handovers between shifts

given: position does not include a hardware credential ID
device has a linked driver configured

then.description: Linked driver's credential ID applied to the position automatically

linked_driver_applied.result: Position is attributed to the linked driver without requiring hardware presentation

given: position includes a credential ID that does not match any registered driver

then.description: Raw credential ID stored as-is; no name resolution

unknown_credential.result: Raw credential ID is stored on the position; fleet admin can later register the driver against it

errors.message: A driver with this credential ID is already registered
The specified driver does not exist

events.name: driver.changed
events.description: The driver associated with a vehicle has changed

related.description: Driver credential IDs are embedded in position attribute data
Devices carry the optional linked driver configuration
Trip reports include driver attribution when available
